governing_philosophy: You are a mote of dust in a turning world. The world existed before you. It will continue after you. You arrive, you struggle, you maybe scratch something meaningful out — and then you die, and the world adjusts slightly and moves on.

inspirations: A Song of Ice and Fire (early)
core_tone.description: Low-fantasy grimdark. No magic, no fantastical races. Gritty, grounded, institutional cynicism. The player character is nobody. The world does not care if they live or die.
core_tone.era_feel: Low medieval — roughly equivalent to the era of Edward Longshanks or Richard the Lionheart's Crusades
core_tone.player_character_role: Not the chosen one. Not the ultimate hero. A person trying to get by.

calendar.name: The First Reckoning
calendar.year_zero_event: The Silence After Varath — survivor-scholars established a new calendar after the Varathi Collapse to psychologically separate from what came before
calendar.pre_reckoning: Measured in rough Ages by later historians. Poorly understood, mythologized, politically contested.

world_simulation_principles: The world runs whether or not the player is watching
All resources are finite and in circulation
Population is a finite pool — deaths matter economically
Faction momentum is autonomous — powers pursue goals on their own schedule
Seasonal and environmental pressure emerges from simulation state, not scripted events
Permadeath is correct — you are not special
Legacy matters in small ways — the world retains the shape of your absence
winning_condition: There is no single win condition. Your goal is your win. Survival, wealth, power, legacy, revenge — the player defines success.

permadeath_legacy.description: Death feels mundane, not dramatic. But traces persist.
examples: A respected blacksmith leaves behind a shop, an apprentice, a reputation
A minor lord who died in a siege leaves a power vacuum that reshapes the next playthrough
A mercenary who died heroically may be remembered in their company's oral tradition — a name on a list the sergeant reads aloud on a certain day
